---
title: "fall2025_mayleen"
author: "Mayleen"
date: "2025-09-04"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, eval = FALSE)
```

# Private Foundation CORE files
```{r} 
source("../SCRIPTS/download_data.R")
tscope_values <- c("501c3") # Download files from Tax Exempt Types in this list; possible values: "501c3", "501ce" 
fscope_values <- c("-pf") # Download files from IRS 990 Form Scope in this list; possible values: "-pz", "-pc", "-pf"
year_values <- seq(from = 1991, to = 2021, by = 1) # Download files from years in this list
download_CORE(tscope_values, fscope_values, year_values)
```

## Exploring Duplicates
```{r, message=F}
library(data.table)
library(readr)
source("../SCRIPTS/clean_helper.R")
source("../SCRIPTS/reviewing_duplicate_EIN.R")

vars.keep <- c("F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START", "EIN2", "PF_01_REV_GRO_PROFIT_BOOKS", "PF_01_REV_TOT_BOOKS", "PF_01_EXP_TOT_EXP_DISBMT_BOOKS", "PF_02_ASSET_TOT_EOY_BV", "PF_02_LIAB_TOT_EOY_BV", "PF_01_EXP_CONTR_PAID_BOOKS", "PF_01_EXP_TOT_EXP_DISBMT_DISBMT")

year_values <- seq(from = 2012, to = 2021, by = 1)
for (year in year_values){
      file_name <- sprintf("../CORE/pf/CORE-%s-501C3-PRIVFOUND-PF-HRMN-V0.csv", year)
      dt <- as.data.table(read_csv(file_name, show_col_types = FALSE))
      
      # vars.keep %in% unique(dt.na_counts$var_name)
      
      dt <- dt[, ..vars.keep]
      setcolorder(dt, c("EIN2", setdiff(names(dt), "EIN2")))
      
      dt.na_counts <- na_counts_df(dt)
      
      dt.dupes.list <- dupes(unique(dt))
      dt.dupes <- dt.dupes.list$dupes
      dt.info <- duplicateEIN2_info(dt, dt.dupes.list$dupes_groups, track.skips = TRUE)
      
      # Print information to file
      cat(year, "NA spread:", quantile(dt.na_counts$na_cnt), "\n", file = "PFdupes.txt", append = TRUE)
      cat(sprintf("The variable with the most NAs is %s (%s).", dt.na_counts$var_name[which.max(dt.na_counts$na_cnt)], max(dt.na_counts$na_cnt)), "\n", file = "PFdupes.txt", append = TRUE)
      
      my_message <- sprintf("In %s, there are %s orgs with duplicate records out of %s, or %s percent.", year, nrow(dt.dupes), length(unique(dt$EIN2)), round(nrow(dt.dupes)/length(unique(dt$EIN2))*100, digits=3))
      cat(my_message, "\n", file = "PFdupes.txt", append = TRUE)
      
      dt.info.df <- dt.info$info |> filter(variable_name != "EIN2")
      diff_col <- dt.info.df$num_differences
      cat(sprintf("There are %s differences in the FISCAL_YEAR_START variable. \nThe variable with the most differences is %s (%s). \nThe variable with the least differences is %s (%s). \nThe total number of differences is %s. The number of those that are due to NAs are %s.", 
                            dt.info.df |> filter(variable_name == "F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START") |> select(num_differences),
                            dt.info.df$variable_name[which.max(diff_col)],
                            max(diff_col),
                            with(subset(dt.info.df, num_differences > 0), variable_name[which.min(num_differences)]),
                            min(diff_col[diff_col > 0]),
                            sum(dt.info.df$num_differences),
                            sum(dt.info.df$num_NA)), "\n", file = "PFdupes.txt", append = TRUE)
      
      cat(sprintf("There were %s orgs with more than 3 duplicates.", dt.info$skips), "\n", file = "PFdupes.txt", append = TRUE)
      cat("===================================================", "\n", file = "PFdupes.txt", append = TRUE)
}
```

## Initial Cleaning of Duplicates

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(data.table)
library(readr)
source("../SCRIPTS/clean_CORE.R")

vars.keep <- c("F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START", "EIN2", "PF_01_REV_GRO_PROFIT_BOOKS", "PF_01_REV_TOT_BOOKS", "PF_01_EXP_TOT_EXP_DISBMT_BOOKS", "PF_02_ASSET_TOT_EOY_BV", "PF_02_LIAB_TOT_EOY_BV", "PF_01_EXP_CONTR_PAID_BOOKS", "PF_01_EXP_TOT_EXP_DISBMT_DISBMT")

year_values <- seq(from = 2021, to = 2021, by = 1)
for (year in year_values){
      # Get data, restrict to variables we care about, remove records that are exact duplicates, and make EIN2 the first column
      file_name <- sprintf("../CORE/pf/CORE-%s-501C3-PRIVFOUND-PF-HRMN-V0.csv", year)
      dt <- as.data.table(read_csv(file_name, show_col_types = FALSE))
      dt <- unique(dt[, ..vars.keep])
      setcolorder(dt, c("EIN2", setdiff(names(dt), "EIN2")))
      
      clean.step1 <-  pf_clean.step1(year, dt)
      clean.step2 <- pf_clean.step2(clean.step1$cleaned_data, clean.step1$unresolved_cases)
      info.dupes <- clean.step2$info
      dt.clean <- clean.step2$final_data
      unresolved <- clean.step2$unresolved_cases
      
      if (nrow(unresolved > 0)){n.unresolved <- length(unique(unresolved$EIN2))} else {n.unresolved <- 0}
      cat(sprintf("Year: %d, num orgs: %d, unresolved orgs: %d, initial unresolved: %d, num records: %d", 
              year, length(unique(dt.clean$EIN2)), n.unresolved, nrow(info.dupes), nrow(dt.clean)),
          "\n", file = "unresolvedPF.txt", append = TRUE)
      cat("===================================================", "\n", file = "unresolvedPF.txt", append = TRUE)
     
      rm(clean.step1, clean.step2) 
}
```

### Example
```{r}
# Load data
dt <- as.data.table(read_csv("example.csv", show_col_types = FALSE))
dt.sol <- as.data.table(read_csv("example_solution.csv", show_col_types = FALSE))

# Define current year for imputation
current_year <- 2013

# Function to count missing values per row
row_na_count <- function(x) rowSums(is.na(x))

# Step 1: Fill missing years if all missing for an org
dt[, FISCAL_YEAR_START := if (all(is.na(FISCAL_YEAR_START))) current_year else FISCAL_YEAR_START,
   by = EIN2]

# Step 2: If some years missing but others not, drop missing
dt <- dt[, .SD[!is.na(FISCAL_YEAR_START) | all(is.na(FISCAL_YEAR_START))], by = EIN2]
# keep row if year not missing OR if all records are missing the year
# drops rows where year missing and at least one record is not missing the year

# Step 3: Resolve duplicates within org-year
dt[, na_count := row_na_count(.SD), .SDcols = !c("EIN2","FISCAL_YEAR_START")]
  
# Mark duplicates to drop
dt[, max_na := max(na_count), by = .(EIN2, FISCAL_YEAR_START)]
dt[, min_na := min(na_count), by = .(EIN2, FISCAL_YEAR_START)]

# If more than one record for org-year, drop record with strictly more missing values
dt_clean <- dt[!(na_count == max_na & max_na > min_na)] # keep row if na_count is not max or there is no actual max / drop row if its na_count is the max and there actually is a max
# TODO: possible issue, what if two are tied for max but there are other records with less... should I keep all or still drop the ones with the most na's

# Step 4: Track unresolved duplicates (ties)
unresolved <- dt_clean[, .N, by = .(EIN2, FISCAL_YEAR_START)][N > 1]

myvec <- colnames(dt.sol)
dt_clean <- dt_clean[, ..myvec]

identical(dt_clean, dt.sol)
```

## Stacking all the years of data
```{r, message=F, warning=F}
library(data.table)
library(readr)

vars.keep <- c("EIN2", "TAX_YEAR", "PF_01_REV_GRO_PROFIT_BOOKS", "PF_01_REV_TOT_BOOKS", "PF_01_EXP_TOT_EXP_DISBMT_BOOKS", "PF_02_ASSET_TOT_EOY_BV", "PF_02_LIAB_TOT_EOY_BV", "PF_01_EXP_CONTR_PAID_BOOKS", "PF_01_EXP_TOT_EXP_DISBMT_DISBMT")

dt.original <- data.table(matrix(ncol=length(vars.keep),nrow=0))
colnames(dt.original) <- vars.keep

dt <- data.table(matrix(ncol=length(vars.keep),nrow=0))
colnames(dt) <- vars.keep

year_values <- seq(from = 1991, to = 2021, by = 1)
for (year in year_values){
      # Get data, restrict to variables we care about, remove records that are exact duplicates, and make EIN2 the first column
      file_name <- sprintf("../CORE/pf/CORE-%s-501C3-PRIVFOUND-PF-HRMN-V0.csv", year)
      
      # load data into environment 
      temp <- as.data.table(read_csv(file_name, show_col_types = FALSE))
      temp <- unique(temp[, ..vars.keep])
      
      # Combine
      dt.original <- rbindlist(list(dt.original, temp))
      dt <- rbindlist(list(dt, temp))
}

source("../SCRIPTS/clean_helper.R")
dt.na_counts <- na_counts_df(dt.original)
rm(temp)

key_var <- c("EIN2", "TAX_YEAR")
# duplicates that need to be resolved
dup_groups <- dt[, .N, by = key_var][N > 1]

# subset duplicate records
dups <- dt[dup_groups, on = key_var]
```


```{r, warning=FALSE}
# Distribution of differences
compare_pair_dt <- function(dt_group) {
      # number of records in this group
      n_records <- nrow(dt_group)
      
      # drop keys (convert to numeric matrix)
      mat <- as.matrix(dt_group)
      
      # names of all columns in the group
      coln <- colnames(dt_group)
      
      # pick out the dollar-valued columns
      dollar_cols <- c("PF_01_REV_GRO_PROFIT_BOOKS", "PF_01_REV_TOT_BOOKS", "PF_01_EXP_TOT_EXP_DISBMT_BOOKS",
                       "PF_02_ASSET_TOT_EOY_BV", "PF_02_LIAB_TOT_EOY_BV", "PF_01_EXP_CONTR_PAID_BOOKS",
                       "PF_01_EXP_TOT_EXP_DISBMT_DISBMT")
      
      # indices of dollar columns
      dollar_idx <- match(dollar_cols, coln)
      
      # compute differences only if there are exactly 2 rows
      if (n_records == 2) {
            r1 <- as.numeric(mat[1, ])
            r2 <- as.numeric(mat[2, ])
            
            diffs <- r1 - r2
            abs_diffs <- abs(diffs)
            
            # check missing-caused differences only for dollar columns
            had_missing_diff <- any(xor(is.na(r1[dollar_idx]), is.na(r2[dollar_idx])))

      } else {
            # for groups with != 2 rows, set diffs to NA
            diffs <- abs_diffs <- numeric(ncol(dt_group))
            diffs[] <- NA
            abs_diffs[] <- NA
            had_missing_diff <- NA
      }
      
      # differences for dollar columns (NA if group != 2 rows)
      diffs_named <- as.list(setNames(diffs[match(dollar_cols, coln)], dollar_cols))
      
      # combine into one data.table row
      data.table(
      n_records      = n_records,                 # <-- new column
      n_diff_cols    = sum(abs_diffs != 0 & !is.na(abs_diffs)),
      n_diff_gt1     = sum(abs_diffs > 1, na.rm = TRUE),
      max_abs_diff   = max(abs_diffs, na.rm = TRUE),
      had_missing_diff = had_missing_diff,
      multi_col_diff = sum(abs_diffs != 0, na.rm = TRUE) > 1
      )[, c(.SD, diffs_named)]
}

diagnostics <- dups[, compare_pair_dt(.SD), by = .(EIN2, TAX_YEAR)] #10060

probs <- seq(0,1,0.1)
quantile(diagnostics$max_abs_diff, probs = probs)
```

```{r}
# identify the dollar-valued columns (the raw diffs you added)
dollar_cols <- c("PF_01_REV_GRO_PROFIT_BOOKS", "PF_01_REV_TOT_BOOKS", "PF_01_EXP_TOT_EXP_DISBMT_BOOKS", "PF_02_ASSET_TOT_EOY_BV", "PF_02_LIAB_TOT_EOY_BV", "PF_01_EXP_CONTR_PAID_BOOKS", "PF_01_EXP_TOT_EXP_DISBMT_DISBMT")

# build the summary table
gt1000_summary <- data.table(
  VAR_NAME = dollar_cols,
  N_GT1000 = sapply(diagnostics[, ..dollar_cols], function(x) sum(abs(x) > 1000, na.rm = TRUE))
)
# groups where differences are all small and there are exactly two rows; exclude cases where there is a difference in one of the financial variables due to a missing variable
insignificant_groups <- diagnostics[max_abs_diff <= 1000 & n_records == 2 & !had_missing_diff, .(EIN2, TAX_YEAR)] #6799

# keep first row within each insignificant group
# add a temporary flag column
dt[, keep := TRUE]  # default: keep everything

# for insignificant groups, keep only the first row per group
dt[insignificant_groups,
          on = .(EIN2, TAX_YEAR),
          keep := seq_len(.N) == 1,
          by = .EACHI]

dt <- dt[keep == TRUE]
dt[, keep := NULL]  # optional: drop temporary column
```

```{r}
# update the duplicates that need to be resolved
dup_groups <- dt[, .N, by = key_var][N > 1] #10060 down to 3261

# subset duplicate records
dups <- dt[dup_groups, on = key_var] # 20173 down to 6575

diagnostics <- dups[, compare_pair_dt(.SD), by = .(EIN2, TAX_YEAR)]

trips <- dups[EIN2 %in% diagnostics[max_abs_diff==-Inf, EIN2]]
trips <- trips[N > 2]

dups.1.col.diff <- dups[EIN2 %in% diagnostics[multi_col_diff == FALSE, EIN2]]

dups.mult.col.diff <- dups[EIN2 %in% diagnostics[multi_col_diff == TRUE, EIN2]]


# Step 1: Find EIN2s with at least one NA in other columns
ein_with_na <- dt[ , if (anyNA(.SD)) .BY$EIN2, by = EIN2]

# Step 2: Extract all rows for those EIN2s
dt_with_na <- dt[EIN2 %in% ein_with_na$V1]

# Inspect
dt_with_na[order(EIN2)]

# This is a duplicate we can resolve by hand because the two records were exactly the same in every column except PF_02_LIAB_TOT_EOY_BV, where the difference was that the value was missing in one of the records but not in the other
dt <- dt[-which(EIN2 == "EIN-26-3424915" & is.na(PF_02_LIAB_TOT_EOY_BV))]
saveRDS(dt, "pf_merged.rds")
```

## Merge with BMF
```{r}
library(data.table)
library(readr)
source("../SCRIPTS/clean_BMF.R")

dt <- readRDS("pf_merged.rds")

unified_bmf <- as.data.frame(read_csv("../CORE/BMF_UNIFIED_V1.1.csv", show_col_types = FALSE))
unified_bmf <- unified_bmf[!(unified_bmf$EIN2 %in% "EIN-00-0000000"),]

#bmf <- as.data.table(read_csv("../CLEAN/cleanBMF.csv", show_col_types = FALSE))
vars_to_keep <- c("EIN2", "NTEEV2", "F990_TOTAL_ASSETS_RECENT","CENSUS_BLOCK_FIPS", "LATITUDE", "LONGITUDE", "CENSUS_COUNTY_NAME", "CENSUS_STATE_ABBR")
thresh <- 7
bmf <- clean_BMF(vars_to_keep, thresh, saveFlag = FALSE)
na_counts <- bmf$na.count
bmf <- bmf$clean.data

# 1. Find organizations only in dt not cleaned bmf
# Use the `key_col` as the join key and perform an anti-join
only_in_dt1_clean_bmf <- dt[!bmf, on = .(EIN2)]

# 2. Count the occurrences of these keys in dt
# Group by `key_col` and count the number of rows (.N)
count_only_in_dt1_clean <- only_in_dt1_clean_bmf[, .N, by = EIN2]

print(nrow(count_only_in_dt1_clean)) # 1166

only_in_dt1_bmf <- dt[!unified_bmf, on = .(EIN2)]
count_only_in_dt1_bmf <- only_in_dt1_bmf[, .N, by = EIN2]
print(nrow(count_only_in_dt1_bmf)) # 1166

nrow(count_only_in_dt1_clean[N >= 3]) # 100

length(intersect(count_only_in_dt1_clean[N >= 7, EIN2], unique(unified_bmf$EIN2))) # 0

merged.dt <- merge(dt, bmf, by = "EIN2")

saveRDS(merged.dt, "pf_merged_bmf.rds")
```

```{r}
library(data.table)
library(readr)

dt <- readRDS("pf_merged_bmf.rds")

key_var <- c("EIN2", "TAX_YEAR")
# duplicates that need to be resolved
dup_groups <- dt[, .N, by = key_var][N > 1]

# subset duplicate records
dups <- dt[dup_groups, on = key_var]

unified_bmf <- as.data.frame(read_csv("../CORE/BMF_UNIFIED_V1.1.csv", show_col_types = FALSE))

unified_bmf$geo
```

## Merging with Census Crosswalk files
```{r}
URL <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/UI-Research/nccs-geo/main/get_census_data.R"
source( URL )

#df  <- get_census_data( geo="msa" )      # 918 metro areas, all years 
df  <- get_census_data( geo="county", years=c(1990,2000,2010, 2019))   # 3,142 counties
#df  <- get_census_data( geo="tract" )    # 72,597 tracts, all years 

# default format is 'long' (stacked years)
#df  <- get_census_data( geo="msa", years=2010:2019 )

# return data in a wide format:
dfw <- get_census_data( geo="county", years=c(1990,2000,2010), format="wide" )

# available years
c(  1990, 2000, 2007, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012,
    2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019  )
```


```{r}
library(data.table)
library(readr)
URL <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/UI-Research/nccs-geo/main/get_census_data.R"
source( URL )

dt <- readRDS("pf_merged_bmf.rds")
dt[, block.census.geoid := sprintf("%015s", CENSUS_BLOCK_FIPS)] 
dt[, 'county.census.geoid' := substr(block.census.geoid, 1, 5)]

blockX <- as.data.table(read_csv("BLOCKX.csv", show_col_types = FALSE))

df  <- get_census_data( geo="county" )   # 3,142 counties, all years 

print(length(unique(dt$block.census.geoid)))
length(intersect(unique(dt$block.census.geoid), blockX$block.census.geoid))

print(length(unique(dt$county.census.geoid)))
length(intersect(unique(dt$county.census.geoid), blockX$county.census.geoid))

missing_ids <- setdiff(unique(dt$county.census.geoid), unique(blockX$county.census.geoid))

missing_ids <- setdiff(missing_ids, c("02066", "00000")) # 02066 is Copper River Census Area in Alaska which did not exist until 2019, thus does not appear in the census crosswalk files (which are standardized to 2010) -- I'm thinking I can manually figure out where to put this record; the rest of the counties are census regions in US territories

dt <- dt[!county.census.geoid %in% missing_ids]

print(length(unique(dt$county.census.geoid)))
length(intersect(unique(dt$county.census.geoid), blockX$county.census.geoid))

# Fixing 38-3737079 and 83-3967403 county geoid... using Census Geocoder found that in 2010 this latitude/longitude corresponded to Valdez-Cordova Census Area, with geoid 02261
dt[EIN2=="EIN-38-3737079", county.census.geoid := "02261"]
dt[EIN2=="EIN-83-3967403", county.census.geoid := "02261"]

print(length(unique(dt$county.census.geoid)))
length(intersect(unique(dt$county.census.geoid), blockX$county.census.geoid))

#unique(df$year)

dt[, DATA_COUNT := .N, by = c("EIN2")]
table(dt$DATA_COUNT)
quantile(dt$DATA_COUNT, probs = seq(0,1,0.1))

tst <- dt[county.census.geoid %in% c("00000")]
tst <- tst[, .SD[1], by = EIN2]

table(tst$DATA_COUNT)
quantile(tst$DATA_COUNT, probs = seq(0,1,0.1))
saveRDS(dt, "pf_processed.rds")
```

```{r}
library(data.table)
library(readr)
URL <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/UI-Research/nccs-geo/main/get_census_data.R"
source( URL )

dt <- readRDS("pf_processed.rds")

blockX <- as.data.table(read_csv("BLOCKX.csv", show_col_types = FALSE))
df  <- get_census_data( geo="county", years = c(2000, 2010))   # 3,142 counties, all years 
setDT(df)

merged_dt <- merge(dt, df,
                   by.x = c("county.census.geoid", "TAX_YEAR"),
                   by.y = c("geoid_2010", "year"),
                   all = FALSE) 
# DOES NOT WORK BECAUSE IN DF YEAR IS CHAR BECAUES RANGE SOMETIMES
```

## Other Processing
```{r}
library(data.table)
library(readr)

pf <- readRDS("pf_processed.rds")
pz <- readRDS("pz_processed.rds")
pz.ce <- readRDS("pz_ce_processed.rds")

# Keep only assets, rev, and expenses variables for private foundations to match PZ
pf <- pf[, !c("PF_01_REV_GRO_PROFIT_BOOKS", "PF_02_LIAB_TOT_EOY_BV", "PF_01_EXP_CONTR_PAID_BOOKS", "PF_01_EXP_TOT_EXP_DISBMT_DISBMT"), with = FALSE]

# rename relevant columns
setnames(pf,
         old = c("PF_02_ASSET_TOT_EOY_BV", "PF_01_REV_TOT_BOOKS", "PF_01_EXP_TOT_EXP_DISBMT_BOOKS", "F990_TOTAL_ASSETS_RECENT"),
         new = c("TOT_ASSET", "TOT_REV", "TOT_EXP", "SIZE"))

pf[, SOURCE := "PF"]
pz[, SOURCE := "PZ"]
pz.ce[, SOURCE := "PZ.CE"]

dt <- rbindlist(list(pf, pz, pz.ce), use.names = TRUE)
dt <- unique(dt)

rm(pf, pz, pz.ce)

# exploring duplicates in the mega dt
source("../SCRIPTS/clean_helper.R")

key_var <- c("EIN2", "TAX_YEAR")
dup_groups <- dt[, .N, by = key_var][N > 1] #
dups <- dt[dup_groups, on = key_var]

dollar_cols <- c("TOT_ASSET", "TOT_REV", "TOT_EXP")
diagnostics <- dups[, compare_pair_dt(.SD, dollar_cols), by = .(EIN2, TAX_YEAR)] #information about the duplicates
probs <- seq(0,1,0.1)
quantile(diagnostics$max_abs_diff, probs = probs)

# There are 370 (EIN2, TAX_YEAR) pairs where the records are exact duplicates EXCEPT in the source column... how to deal with?

dt[, DATA_COUNT := .N, by = c("EIN2")] #recalculate because now may have from different sources... but will be overcount because of duplicates
quantile(dt$DATA_COUNT, probs = probs)

saveRDS(dt, "mega.rds")
```


```{r}
# Distribution of records per org pair
dt[, DATA_COUNT := .N, by = c("EIN2")]
table(dt$DATA_COUNT)
quantile(dt$DATA_COUNT, probs = seq(0,1,0.1))
# 90% of the orgs have more than 6 records, so I think its okay to drop those with 6 or less
pf_orgs <- unique(dt$EIN2)

df <- readRDS("../PREPROCESSING_mays/df.rds")
pz_orgs <- unique(df$EIN2)

orgs_both <- intersect(pf_orgs, pz_orgs)

setDT(df)

# look at shared orgs
pf_shared <- dt[EIN2 %in% orgs_both, .(EIN2, TAX_YEAR)]
pz_shared <- df[EIN2 %in% orgs_both, .(EIN2, TAX_YEAR)]

# exact matches (org,year) - these are, once again, duplicates that need to be resolved
overlap <- pf_shared[pz_shared, on = .(EIN2, TAX_YEAR), nomatch=0L]
length(unique(overlap$EIN2)) #2203

# Get data_counts across all orgs so far
count.pf <- dt[, .(n.pf = .N), by = EIN2]
count.pz <- df[, .(n.pz = .N), by = EIN2]

counts.all <- merge(count.pf, count.pz, by = "EIN2", all = TRUE)
counts.all[is.na(n.pf), n.pf := 0]
counts.all[is.na(n.pz), n.pz := 0]

counts.all[, DATA_COUNT := n.pf + n.pz]

quantile(dt$DATA_COUNT, probs = seq(0,1,0.1))
quantile(df$DATA_COUNT, probs = seq(0,1,0.1))
quantile(counts.all$DATA_COUNT, probs = seq(0,1,0.1))
# It looks like by getting rid of organizations with 6 or less records, we will be losing 40% of organizations. Possibly by adding in the remaining PZ data this will change. 
```


# SHELDUS Data

```{r}
library(data.table)
library(readr)

# stnd2015, 1991-2020
dt <- as.data.table(read_csv("sheldus/direct_loss_aggregated_output_25432.csv", show_col_types = FALSE))

# Checking for duplicates
key_var <- c("County_FIPS", "Year", "Hazard")
dup_groups <- disaster[, .N, by = key_var][N > 1]
dups <- disaster[dup_groups, on = key_var]

# We can safely remove the records where CountyName begins with an asterick because those are "historical" counties that no longer exist
dups <- dups[substr(CountyName, 1, 1) == "*"]
disaster <- fsetdiff(disaster, dups[, !c("N"), with = FALSE])

setnames(disaster,
         old = c("County_FIPS", "Year"),
         new = c("county.census.geoid", "TAX_YEAR"))

#saveRDS(disaster, "disasters.rds")
```

```{r}
library(data.table)
library(readr)

# stnd2010, 1990-2021
dt <- as.data.table(read_csv("sheldus/direct_loss_aggregated_output_27826.csv", show_col_types = FALSE))

# Checking for duplicates
key_var <- c("County_FIPS", "Year")
dup_groups <- dt[, .N, by = key_var][N > 1]
dups <- dt[dup_groups, on = key_var]

# We can safely remove the records where CountyName begins with an asterick because those are "historical" counties that no longer exist
dups <- dups[substr(CountyName, 1, 1) == "*"]
dt <- fsetdiff(dt, dups[, !c("N"), with = FALSE])

setnames(dt,
         old = c("County_FIPS", "Year"),
         new = c("county.geoid", "TAX_YEAR"))

saveRDS(dt, "disasters.rds")
```



# Scratchwork
```{r, message=F}
library(data.table)
library(readr)
source("../SCRIPTS/clean_helper.R")
source("../SCRIPTS/reviewing_duplicate_EIN.R")

dt.2011 <- as.data.table(read_csv(sprintf("../CORE/pf/CORE-%s-501C3-PRIVFOUND-PF-HRMN-V0.csv", 2011), show_col_types = FALSE))

dt.2012 <- as.data.table(read_csv(sprintf("../CORE/pf/CORE-%s-501C3-PRIVFOUND-PF-HRMN-V0.csv", 2012), show_col_types = FALSE))

dt.2013 <- as.data.table(read_csv(sprintf("../CORE/pf/CORE-%s-501C3-PRIVFOUND-PF-HRMN-V0.csv", 2013), show_col_types = FALSE))

vars.keep <- c("F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START", "EIN2", "PF_01_REV_GRO_PROFIT_BOOKS", "PF_01_REV_TOT_BOOKS", "PF_01_EXP_TOT_EXP_DISBMT_BOOKS", "PF_02_ASSET_TOT_EOY_BV", "PF_02_LIAB_TOT_EOY_BV", "PF_01_EXP_CONTR_PAID_BOOKS", "PF_01_EXP_TOT_EXP_DISBMT_DISBMT")
               
dt.2011 <- dt.2011[, ..vars.keep]
dt.2012 <- dt.2012[, ..vars.keep]
dt.2013 <- dt.2013[, ..vars.keep]

dt.2011 <- unique(dt.2011)
dt.2012 <- unique(dt.2012)
dt.2013 <- unique(dt.2013)

setcolorder(dt.2011, c("EIN2", setdiff(names(dt.2011), "EIN2")))
setcolorder(dt.2012, c("EIN2", setdiff(names(dt.2012), "EIN2")))
setcolorder(dt.2013, c("EIN2", setdiff(names(dt.2013), "EIN2")))

na.2011 <- na_counts_df(dt.2011)
na.2012 <- na_counts_df(dt.2012)
na.2013 <- na_counts_df(dt.2013)

dlist.2011 <- dupes(dt.2011)
dlist.2012 <- dupes(dt.2012)
dlist.2013 <- dupes(dt.2013)

dupes.2011 <- dlist.2011$dupes
dupes.2012 <- dlist.2012$dupes
dupes.2013 <- dlist.2013$dupes

groups.2011 <- dlist.2011$dupes_groups
groups.2012 <- dlist.2012$dupes_groups
groups.2013 <- dlist.2013$dupes_groups

rm(dlist.2011, dlist.2012, dlist.2013)

info.2011 <- duplicateEIN2_info(dt.2011, groups.2011, track.skips = TRUE)
info.2012 <- duplicateEIN2_info(dt.2012, groups.2012, track.skips = TRUE)
info.2013 <- duplicateEIN2_info(dt.2013, groups.2013, track.skips = TRUE)

mult.2013 <- (dupes.2013 |> filter(n > 3))$EIN2
sub.2013 <- dt.2013[EIN2 %in% mult.2013]
```

```{r}
# STEP ONE: When an organization is always missing the fiscal year, but has unique tax years for each record, use the tax year to fill in the fiscal year
# Which orgs have all FISCAL_YEAR variables missing?
org_missing_status <- dt[, .(
  all_missing_fys = all(is.na(F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START)),
  n_total = .N,
  n_missing = sum(is.na(F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START))
), by = EIN2]

always_missing <- org_missing_status[all_missing_fys==TRUE, EIN2]

tax_year_summary_by_org <- tax_year_summary[EIN2 %in% always_missing, .(
  has_dup_tax_year = any(is_dup_tax_year),   # TRUE if org ever had a dup tax year
  n_total_tax_years = .N,                    # total tax years observed for this org
  n_dup_tax_years = sum(is_dup_tax_year)     # number of tax years with duplicates
), by = EIN2]

# EINs where fiscal year is always missing AND tax years are unique; fills in 63334 missing values
dt[, IMPUTED_FISCAL_WITH_TAX := FALSE] # flag to keep track of where fiscal years are imputed using the tax year

safe <- tax_year_summary_by_org[n_dup_tax_years == 0, EIN2]
dt[EIN2 %in% safe & is.na(F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START), `:=`(
      F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START = TAX_YEAR,
      IMPUTED_FISCAL_WITH_TAX = TRUE
)]

sum(is.na(dt$F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START)) # 774,178 missing fy

rm(safe, org_missing_status, always_missing)
sum(dt$IMPUTED_FISCAL_WITH_TAX)
```


```{r}
# Step : Impute missing fiscal years with tax year whenever the record missing the fiscal year has a unique tax year (no other records for that organization share the same tax year), but only where there is a strong pattern: if 100% of the non-missing fiscal years for that organization match the corresponding tax year, we can set FISCAL_YEAR = TAX_YEAR; however if 100% of records with non-missing fiscal years have a fiscal year equal to TAX_YEAR-1, then we set FISCAL = TAX -1; otherwise leave alone

# figure out which ones to modify
org_pattern_summary <- dt[, .(
  n_total = .N,
  n_missing_fys = sum(is.na(F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START)),
  n_equal = sum(F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START == TAX_YEAR, na.rm = TRUE),
  n_not_equal = sum(!is.na(F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START) &
                      F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START != TAX_YEAR)
), by = EIN2]

# Add proportions based on non-missing cases
org_pattern_summary[, n_non_missing := n_total - n_missing_fys]
org_pattern_summary[, prop_equal := fifelse(n_non_missing > 0,
                                            n_equal / n_non_missing, NA_real_)]
org_pattern_summary[, prop_not_equal := fifelse(n_non_missing > 0,
                                                n_not_equal / n_non_missing, NA_real_)]

# Join summary info onto main data.table
dt <- merge(
  dt,
  org_pattern_summary[, .(EIN2, prop_equal, prop_not_equal)],
  by = "EIN2",
  all.x = TRUE
)

# Merge EIN × TAX_YEAR duplicate info
dt <- merge(
  dt,
  tax_year_summary[, .(EIN2, TAX_YEAR, is_dup_tax_year)],
  by = c("EIN2", "TAX_YEAR"),
  all.x = TRUE
)

# Case 1: org always fiscal = tax_year, but only if tax_year is unique
dt[is.na(F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START) & prop_equal == 1 & !is_dup_tax_year,
  `:=`(F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START = TAX_YEAR, 
       IMPUTED_FISCAL_WITH_TAX = TRUE
  )]

# Case 2: org always fiscal = tax_year - 1, but only if tax_year is unique
dt[is.na(F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START) & prop_not_equal == 1 & !is_dup_tax_year,
  `:=`(F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START = TAX_YEAR - 1,
       IMPUTED_FISCAL_WITH_TAX = TRUE
  )]

# after <- dt[EIN2 %in% eins_to_check, c("EIN2", "F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START", "TAX_YEAR")]
# setorder(after, EIN2, TAX_YEAR)

sum(is.na(dt$F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START)) # 542,892 missing fy
```

```{r}
# Step 1: Identify groups of potential duplicates (same EIN2, TAX_YEAR, and all other cols except fiscal year)
cols_to_check <- setdiff(
  names(dt),
  c("EIN2", "TAX_YEAR", "F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START")
)

dt[, dup_group := .GRP, by = c("EIN2", "TAX_YEAR", cols_to_check)]

# Step 2: Summarize each group
dup_summary <- dt[
  ,
  .(
    n_total = .N,
    n_na = sum(is.na(F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START)),
    n_non_na = sum(!is.na(F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START))
  ),
  by = dup_group
]

# Step 3: Count how many groups match the pattern (2 rows: 1 NA, 1 non-NA)
n_exact_pairs <- dup_summary[n_total == 2 & n_na == 1 & n_non_na == 1, .N]

# Optional: also count how many *rows* this represents (should be 2 × n_exact_pairs)
n_exact_rows <- dup_summary[n_total == 2 & n_na == 1 & n_non_na == 1, sum(n_total)]

nrow(dt) - n_exact_pairs # 2,100,524

# Step 1: Identify duplicate rows except for fiscal year

# Step 2: Flag duplicates
dt[, dup_group := .GRP, by = c("EIN2", "TAX_YEAR", cols_to_check)]

# Step 3: Within each dup_group, if exactly one NA fiscal year and one non-NA → drop the NA
rows_to_drop <- dt[
  , .(
    n_total = .N,
    n_na = sum(is.na(F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START)),
    n_non_na = sum(!is.na(F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START))
  ),
  by = dup_group
][n_total == 2 & n_na == 1 & n_non_na == 1, dup_group]

# Step 4: Remove the NA record from those groups
dt <- dt[!(dup_group %in% rows_to_drop & is.na(F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START))]

# Step 5: Clean up helper column
# dt[, dup_group := NULL]

#dt[dup_group %in% rows_to_drop, c("EIN2", "TAX_YEAR", "F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START", "PF_01_REV_GRO_PROFIT_BOOKS", "PF_02_ASSET_TOT_EOY_BV", "dup_group")]

sum(is.na(dt$F9_00_FISCAL_YEAR_START)) # 7700 missing fy
```
